title: coleotrype goudotii var. typica
intro: Coleotrype goudotii var. typica, also known as the Coleotrype goudotii, is a species of plant in the family Malvaceae, native to Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Coleotrype goudotii var. typica typically involve drying and infusing the leaves or roots, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically as a paste or ointment.
